behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so summaries can attribute statements to attendees.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is not just parser logic; it is whether the real STT model preserves diarization labels. REAL-INFRA exists (real API call), but LLM-DEP rule for AI/ML output quality applies first: correctness depends on model output content, so Agentic is the minimum. Rejected adjacent lower (System): behavior text requires label preservation quality ("preserves speaker labels"), not only transport/HTTP behavior. Rejected adjacent higher (Workflow): behavior text is scoped to STT output itself, not a required sequence of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Send a real multi-speaker meeting audio file with known speaker turns to the production STT API and assert transcript segments retain speaker labels consistently enough to map utterances to the correct attendees.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is local parsing/structuring logic; failure boundary is one function/object transforming transcript text to structured fields. No real OS/network/subprocess dependency and no model-quality dependency are stated. Rejected adjacent higher (Integration): text says "Transcript parsing extracts..." and does not require cross-module state handoff to expose the bug. Adjacent lower (L-1) is not applicable below Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript lines containing explicit assignments (e.g., "Alex will prepare the budget") into the parser function and assert exact assignee/task extraction into the summary input schema.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test directly exercises the stated parsing boundary; no higher infra required for this behavior.

behaviors.description: Live summary includes at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task.
behaviors.justification: Behavior checks semantic completeness of generated summary content. Even with deterministic assertions, correctness depends on what the real summarization model decides to include for each attendee. Rejected adjacent lower (System): real HTTP/process checks alone cannot validate model decision quality for coverage. Rejected adjacent higher (Workflow): this can be tested by supplying a prepared transcript directly to the summarizer, so multiple real agentic stages are not strictly required.
behaviors.test_description: Use a transcript fixture with known per-attendee assignments, call the real summarization LLM, and assert each attendee with an assignment appears in at least one produced action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into `decisions` schema fields, not generic narrative.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is deterministic routing/mapping logic after decisions are already identified. No required external infra or model judgment in this behavior text. Rejected adjacent higher (Integration): text is explicitly about "logic routes identified decisions," which is local transformation logic. Adjacent lower (L-1) is not applicable below Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Given an input structure containing pre-identified decisions, assert the mapper places them into `decisions` fields and leaves narrative fields free of those decision objects.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test remains at the routing function boundary, matching Unit.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in transcript appear in live summary `decisions` section, not buried in narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is about whether the real summarization model surfaces decision content in the right section; mocks can hide the failure by returning ideal structured outputs. Rejected adjacent lower (System): transport and API uptime do not verify decision-placement quality. Rejected adjacent higher (Workflow): behavior can be validated with transcript-to-summary in one model step without requiring multi-agentic chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcripts with explicit decision statements to the real summarizer and assert those decisions are present in output `decisions` entries and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: For audio longer than 60 minutes, service splits into sequential segments and merges into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequential segmented processing plus merged coherence, which is multiple agentic steps in sequence (per-segment transcription/summarization + merge). Rejected adjacent lower (Agentic): single-step model testing cannot expose cross-segment handoff/merge failures implied by "segments... sequentially, merging... coherent summary." Adjacent higher (L+1) is not applicable above Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production pipeline segmentation; verify all segments are processed in order and final merged summary preserves cross-segment action items/decisions without duplication or contradiction.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The described test requires chained model outputs and merge behavior, matching Workflow.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline (upload, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes end-to-end on a real meeting recording without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly full-pipeline and includes multiple model-dependent stages (STT + LLM summarization) plus orchestration/delivery. This is beyond single-step agentic and beyond pure system infra checks. Rejected adjacent lower (Agentic): one model call cannot validate end-to-end autonomous completion across all stages named in behavior text. Adjacent higher (L+1) is not applicable above Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run with a real meeting audio file in production-like environment and assert successful unattended completion through upload, transcription, summarization, and structured output delivery.
